zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune於1060年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯貝圖內時，他的父親Robert DE BETHUNE25歲，母親Giselberthe D’Halluin16歲。他和Alix de Peronne至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他在法國帕-德-卡拉斯阿拉斯被列為avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras。他去世於1128年在法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯沙吉莫享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune于1060年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯貝圖內时，他的父亲Robert DE BETHUNE25岁，母亲Giselberthe D’Halluin16岁。他和Alix de Peronne至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。他在法国帕-德-卡拉斯阿拉斯被列为avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras。他去世于1128年在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯沙吉莫享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bethune
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin); French (Béthune): habitational name from Béthune in Pas-de-Calais, Picardy, France, recorded in the 8th century in the Latin form Bitunia, probably an adjective (with villa understood) derived from the oblique form of an ancient Germanic personal name Betto, an assimilated form of Berto (see Bert ). See also Scottish Beaton .

variants.name: Beaton
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Béthune in Pas-de-Calais, France (see Bethune ). The Hebridean family of McBeth , hereditary physicians, adopted the surname Beaton in the 16th century.
English: from the Middle English personal name Beton, a diminutive of Bete, a pet form of Beatrice (see Beatrice ). Compare Beat .
English: variant of   Betton  .

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: "le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Profit’ or ‘sharp’.
2  ♀, ♂ ‘Upright’.

nameMeanings.name: Gros"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Rosalind   and   Rosamund .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune was born in 1060, in Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, his father, Robert DE BETHUNE, was 25 and his mother, Giselberthe D’Halluin, was 16. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Alix de Peronne. His occupation is listed as avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras in Arras, Pas-de-Calais, France. He died in 1128, in Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune est né en 1060, à Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Robert DE BETHUNE, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Giselberthe D’Halluin, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Alix de Peronne. Sa profession de son indiquée est avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras à Arras, Pas-de-Calais, France. Il est décédé en 1128, à Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune 1060 in Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert DE BETHUNE 25 und seine Mutter Giselberthe D’Halluin 16. Er hatte mit Alix de Peronne mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. sein Beruf ist als avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras in Arras, Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich aufgeführt. Er starb 1128 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune è nato il nel 1060 nel Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Robert DE BETHUNE, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Giselberthe D’Halluin, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Alix de Peronne. La sua occupazione è indicata come avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras nel Arras, Pas-de-Calais, Francia. Morì nel 1128, nel Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethuneが1060年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスブeスウンで生まれた時，父Robert DE BETHUNEは25歳，母Giselberthe D’Halluinは16歳であった。彼はAlix de Peronneとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。フランスプアス-デ-カレイスアラスでの職業はavoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arrasと記録されている。彼は1128年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスサイント-オマーで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune(이)가 1060년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 브e툰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert DE BETHUNE(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Giselberthe D’Halluin(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Alix de Peronne(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 프랑스 파스-데-캘레이 아라스에서 avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras이었다. 그는 1128년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 세인트-머에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune nasceu em 1060, em Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Robert DE BETHUNE, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Giselberthe D’Halluin, tinha 16 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Alix de Peronne. Sua profissão aparece como avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras em Arras, Pas-de-Calais, França. Ele faleceu em 1128, em Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune родился в 1060 г. в местности бeтун, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert DE BETHUNE было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Giselberthe D’Halluin, 16. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Alix de Peronne.  Он работал по профессии avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras в местности аррас, пас-де-калеи, Франция. Он умер в 1128 г. в местности сеинт-омир, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert "le Gros" IV de Bethune nació en 1060, en Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Robert DE BETHUNE, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Giselberthe D’Halluin, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Alix de Peronne. Su ocupación registrada era avoué, protecteur de saint vaast d'arras en Arras, Pas-de-Calais, Francia. Murió en 1128, en Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 68 años.
